items.title: The greyhound races Tasmanian authorities don't want you to see

children.content: If you watched greyhound race videos on Tasracing's website, you would likely never know that dozens of dogs were injured or killed during events last year.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: 'They're often not heard': Hip hop helping kids find words for the feelings

children.content: A Tasmanian youth outreach program is providing free hip hop writing workshops for young people, which experts say could be "life-changing".

image.altText: Kieran's hand as he writes on white paper, resting on a brown table. He wears a purple hoodie and his hand has tattoos.

items.title: Shyanne Lee-Tatnell's accused murderer likely to face trial next year

children.content: A man who has pleaded not guilty to murdering 14-year-old Tasmanian teenager Shyanne Lee-Tatnell in 2023 will likely stand trial early next year.

image.altText: A young blonde girl smiles at the camera

items.title: Tasmania moves to ban 'disgraceful' non-consensual sharing of sexual imagery

children.content: Tasmania catches up with other jurisdictions with plans to make it an offence to share sexually explicit images including "revenge porn" and AI-generated deepfakes.

gallery.altText: Two women stand together in a park, smiling.

thumbnail.altText: Two women stand together in a park, smiling.

items.title: Plans for DarkLab Transformer artwork ditched amid rising costs

children.content: The group behind Tasmania's Dark Mofo festival has scrapped plans to develop a "destination public artwork" in the state's south-east due to escalating costs.

image.altText: Mirrored buildings on the side of a hill

items.title: Aboriginal activist takes on logging company staff during trespass court case

children.content: An Aboriginal activist has interrogated forestry company staff over their knowledge of cultural heritage as part of her defence against a trespass charge.

image.altText: Ruth Langford, wearing a white jacket, standing in front of the Hobart Magistrates Court smiling

items.title: Tougher penalties, but no gun limit under Tasmania's proposed new laws

children.content: New gun laws, which create tougher penalties for the theft and possession of stolen firearms, but do not include gun caps, have been unveiled by the Tasmanian government, asking for public feedback.

thumbnail.altText: Collection of seized and stolen firearms

items.title: International experts weigh in on Australia’s bird flu response

children.content: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

articles.title: Surveillance increased on reef island after bird flu arrival

children.content: A vulnerable colony of red-tailed tropicbirds is being monitored for the deadly H5 bird flu, with fears the impact on Australian native bird populations could be significant.

image.altText: A fluffy white bird with a black beak

articles.title: First case of deadly H5N1 bird flu confirmed in NSW

children.content: The first case of the deadly H5 bird flu is confirmed in a giant petrel found on the NSW Mid North Coast.

items.title: Taylor Swift's wedding a lesson in 'media mastery'

children.content: After mounting speculation, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have officially tied the knot. One expert says what we know, and don't know, about the wedding is a lesson in "media mastery".

items.title: Woman denied bail after child found dead in NSW Central Coast home

items.title: For 60 years, Play School has never broken this rule

children.content: Australian Story is given rare behind-the-scenes access to Play School as it celebrates 60 years on air. An intimate look at the creative process of the iconic children's TV show that's still guided by one golden rule.

items.title: Eels and Sea Eagles score after back-to-back trick plays

children.content: Dylan Walker throws a miraculous behind-the-back pass for the Eels to score, before Reuben Garrick's onside kick allows the Sea Eagles to retake the lead early in the second half. Follow live.

items.title: Winmar statue removed during NAIDOC Week after assault conviction

children.content: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

items.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration

children.content: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

items.title: The spearfisherman who rediscovered a shipwreck

children.content: The startling noise of metal on metal was unusual on a coral reef and unlocked a nearly century-old mystery of a tragic maritime disaster, sunken treasure and a peculiar, inscribed turtle shell.

image.altText: Black-and-white image of the sinking of the SS Gothenburg during a storm.

items.title: 'Breach bail, go to jail': Premier promises harsher youth crime laws

children.content: The expanded "adult crime, adult time" laws would see youth offenders who commit serious crimes while on bail facing a mandatory jail sentence.

items.title: Socceroos match shows where Labor's gambling ad laws fall short

items.title: Australian influencer Lily Jay's tangled web of AI manipulation
